findings.title: safeFetch followed 3xx redirects without re-validating each hop
findings.summary: Node's built-in fetch follows Location: blindly. A target that 302s to 169.254.169.254 (or any private IP) bypassed validateScanTarget after the first hop.
findings.fix: Manual redirect loop with per-hop validateScanTarget; cross-host redirects rejected outright; same-host redirects capped at 5 hops.

findings.title: startCheckoutSession accepted userId from client (IDOR)
findings.summary: The userId parameter was stamped into Stripe metadata, letting any logged-in user upgrade a victim's plan by paying.
findings.fix: Dropped the parameter. userId is now derived server-side from getSession().

findings.title: Password change did not invalidate other sessions
findings.summary: A stolen session cookie remained valid for the full 7-day TTL even after the legitimate user rotated their password.
findings.fix: Mirror reset-password: deleteAllSessions + createSession(current).

findings.title: TOTP seed stored plaintext at rest
findings.summary: users.totp_secret held the base32 TOTP seed unencrypted. DB read = full 2FA bypass.
findings.fix: Route through encryptApiKey/decryptApiKey (AES-256-GCM). Added idempotent backfill on startup.

findings.title: Discord OAuth tokens stored plaintext at rest
findings.summary: discord_connections.access_token and refresh_token were plaintext. DB read = Discord account takeover via refresh token.
findings.fix: Encrypt via encryptApiKey; added getDiscordTokens() helper that decrypts inline; backfill migration on startup.

findings.title: DISABLE_CSP=1 was shipped in production env
findings.summary: Operator-set env var stripped every security header (CSP, COOP, CORP, XFO, Permissions-Policy, HSTS) silently.
findings.fix: env validator refuses DISABLE_CSP=1 when NODE_ENV=production. Removed from .env.local.

findings.title: No Origin/Referer check on mutating API routes
findings.summary: Cross-site POST could ride the session cookie and change the victim's email.
findings.fix: Middleware enforces Origin match + Sec-Fetch-Site in same-origin/same-site on every mutating /api/v3/ request. Webhooks and Discord callback are explicitly exempt (signed-payload trust boundary).

findings.title: API-key daily cap race: concurrent requests bypass the limit
findings.summary: checkRateLimit + recordUsage were two separate statements. N concurrent requests at used=limit-1 all passed, all inserted.
findings.fix: Single transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on api_keys.id; recordUsage() is now a no-op kept only for back-compat.

findings.title: Docker base + compose tag both mutable
findings.summary: node:22-alpine and ghcr.io/vulnradar/vulnradar:latest are moving targets; a compromised registry push immediately ships to every self-hoster.
findings.fix: Pinned to node:22.11.0-alpine (self-hosters can replace with @sha256:<digest>). Compose uses :3.0.0. Added tini for PID 1 signal forwarding + HEALTHCHECK.

findings.title: Login timing oracle leaks registered emails
findings.summary: No-user path returned ~5-20ms; user-exists path ran scrypt (80-200ms). Observable delta.
findings.fix: No-user path runs verifyPassword against a precomputed dummy hash to equalize timing.

findings.title: account/delete blocked by FK violation on admin_audit_log
findings.summary: admin_audit_log.target_user_id and access_rules.created_by had no ON DELETE clause; user self-delete returned 500.
findings.fix: Self-healing idempotent migration adds fk_admin_audit_target_user ON DELETE SET NULL and fk_access_rules_created_by ON DELETE SET NULL on startup.

findings.title: access-rules catch block failed open
findings.summary: DB error in blacklist query silently allowed every scan through — turning a DB outage into a blacklist bypass.
findings.fix: Catch block now returns { allowed: false } — fail-closed.

findings.title: admin_audit_log.details stored raw user emails (365-day retention)
findings.summary: Emails leaked in audit log long after the user changed or deleted their address.
findings.fix: Central logAuditAction helper masks any email-like substring via redactEmailsInDetails before insert.

findings.title: SMTP transport fell back to plaintext
findings.summary: secure:false without requireTLS meant misconfigured relays could send password-reset + 2FA codes in cleartext.
findings.fix: Port 465 -> secure:true. Port 587/25 -> requireTLS:true. Min TLSv1.2.

findings.title: Audit-log IPs read leftmost X-Forwarded-For (spoofable)
findings.summary: 7 routes trusted the client-supplied IP without consulting TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDR.
findings.fix: Replace with await getClientIp() everywhere.

findings.title: No URL length cap at scan API boundary
findings.summary: CONFIG_MAX_URL_LENGTH was defined but never enforced — a 50MB URL string hit DNS resolution and DB INSERT.
findings.fix: Reject with 400 before any DNS / DB work when url.length > SCANNING.MAX_URL_LENGTH.

findings.title: scan_history.response_headers stored Set-Cookie values
findings.summary: Scan-target session cookies persisted to DB indefinitely.
findings.fix: redactSensitiveResponseHeaders strips Set-Cookie / Cookie / Authorization / etc. before insert.

findings.title: Bulk scan limit hardcoded to 10 instead of MAX_URLS_IN_BULK (100)
findings.summary: Self-hosters configuring MAX_URLS_IN_BULK got silent bypass.
findings.fix: Use SCANNING.MAX_URLS_IN_BULK.

findings.title: browser/sessions had no per-user cap (financial DoS)
findings.summary: A compromised session could rack up real BrowserBase costs by spawning unlimited sessions.
findings.fix: Added RATE_LIMITS.browserSession (20/hour per user).

findings.title: window_start at ms precision created a fresh bucket per request
findings.summary: ON CONFLICT(key, window_start) matched exact ms; attacker firing N requests in 1ms got N buckets at count=1.
findings.fix: Quantize window_start to floor(epoch_ms / window_ms) * window_ms.
